description:
Pure function lowering fz type syntax into a Descr (set-theoretic type lattice from src/types.rs).

## Signature

```
pub fn parse_type_expr(tokens: &[Token], type_env: &ModuleTypeEnv) -> Result<Descr, Diag>
```

(type_env hookup lands in .31.3; for this ticket pass an empty env.)

## Grammar

- Scalars: `nil`, `bool`, `integer`, `float`, `binary`, `atom`
- Atom literal: `:foo`
- Integer/float literals: `42`, `3.14` (singleton Descrs)
- List: `[T]`
- Tuple: `{T, U, V}`
- Map (open): `%{required(K) => V, optional(K2) => V2}` — pick a syntax; minimum: `%{}` for any map
- Function: `(A, B) -> R` (zero-arg: `() -> R`)
- Union: `T | U` (right-associative or left, document)
- Top: `_` or `any` (Descr::any())
- Named ref: bare identifier (resolved against type_env)

## Tests

- Each grammar form parses to expected Descr (use Descr equality)
- Union precedence: `integer | float | nil` parses as a single 3-way union
- Function arity: `() -> integer`, `(integer) -> integer`, `(integer, float) -> binary`
- Errors: malformed type, unknown name (when type_env is empty: defer that error to .31.3? or error here?), trailing tokens
- Round-trip with types.rs Descr Display where lossless

## Out of scope

- Parameterized aliases like `result(t)` — deferred
- Opaque types — deferred
- Integration with the main expression parser — separate concern; this is a standalone module-level function
title: Type-expression parser → Descr
